Enterprise AI & Automation Lead




Location: Melbourne or Brisbane

Steel Builder International (SBI) is in a strong national growth phase, delivering high-quality structural steel solutions for industrial, commercial, infrastructure and mining projects across Australia. As the business expands, we are investing in enterprise AI and automation to improve operational performance, accelerate delivery and strengthen commercial outcomes across fabrication, supply chain and project delivery.

We are currently seeking a commercially focused Enterprise AI & Automation Lead to drive the practical application of artificial intelligence and automation across our business.

This role focuses on deploying applied AI solutions that improve real operational workflows across fabrication, supply chain, estimating, project delivery and corporate functions. The role will define and execute SBI's AI strategy while delivering measurable outcomes including margin protection, rework reduction, improved forecasting and faster delivery cycles.

This is a unique opportunity to build and lead AI capability in a major construction and fabrication environment where technology directly impacts real projects, real delivery timelines and real commercial outcomes.

About the Role

Reporting to the General Manager – Commercial & Contracts, the Enterprise AI & Automation Lead will identify, develop and implement AI and automation initiatives that improve how SBI operates across the enterprise.

You will work closely with operations, fabrication, finance, and commercial teams to embed AI-enabled processes that reduce manual effort and strengthen project and commercial performance.

Key responsibilities include:

Defining and executing SBI's enterprise AI strategy, governance and roadmap

Identifying opportunities to automate and optimise workflows across fabrication, logistics, estimating, procurement and project delivery

Enhancing commercial intelligence, forecasting accuracy and tender insights

Uplifting enterprise data architecture, ERP integration and governance maturity

Evaluating and implementing AI tools, platforms and automation technologies

Leading pilot programs and scaling solutions that deliver measurable ROI

About You

You are a commercially minded technology leader who understands how to apply AI and automation in practical operational environments.

You will likely bring:

Experience implementing AI, automation or advanced analytics solutions

A minimum of 8+ years of experience implementing AI/automation solutions

Strong understanding of process optimisation and system integration

Ability to translate technical innovation into measurable business outcomes

Experience working across operations, commercial and technology teams

A pragmatic, outcome-focused approach to innovation

Experience in construction, fabrication or other asset-intensive environments will be highly regarded.

Qualifications

Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Information Systems or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience

Proven experience deploying AI or automation solutions into production environments that deliver measurable operational or commercial outcomes

Experience integrating AI or advanced analytics solutions with enterprise systems such as ERP, estimating, procurement or operational platforms

Hands-on implementation experience with ERP system integration for AI and advanced analytics solutions

Demonstrated ability to deliver technology initiatives that improve operational performance, efficiency, forecasting or commercial decision-making

Experience working with enterprise data platforms, data governance frameworks and secure data environments

Certifications or professional development in AI, machine learning, cloud platforms, data engineering or automation technologies (desirable)

What Success Looks Like

Success in this role will be measured through outcomes such as:

Margin protection and commercial improvements driven by AI initiatives

Reduction in rework and manual processes

Faster estimating, RFI and shop drawing cycles

Improved forecasting accuracy and commercial insight

Adoption and sustained use of AI tools across the business

Why Join SBI

Opportunity to shape enterprise AI capability from the ground up

Work directly with senior leadership on high-impact innovation

Deliver technology that transforms how major projects are delivered




Apply

If you are passionate about applying AI to real operational challenges and delivering measurable business outcomes, we encourage you to apply.

Key Responsibilities:

Nearby Melbourne CBD location (3-4 days in office).

New Hardware + Software Product

We are developing a new hardware–software system built around tactile, non-visual interaction and voice-assisted AI.

An MVP prototype already exists. The next phase is to refine, stabilise and structure the software so it can support manufacturing, pilot deployment, and future integrations with external platforms and technologies.

The product combines custom tactile hardware with a companion application. A hardware engineer is leading device electronics and firmware. We are seeking a senior software engineer to lead the software architecture, system integration and production-ready software development required to move from MVP to a manufacturable product.


Engagement Details

Rate: $130-150/hr (exc. GST) depending on experience
Duration: 8–10 weeks
Time commitment: Initial ~15-20 hours/week
Location: Sydney-based preferred
Working style: Weekly syncs, async collaboration, fast decision cycles
In-person: ~8 hours in person (spread across two days), remainder remote
Start: Immediate

Australian work rights are required for this contract position.

The technology stack is flexible. The engineer will be trusted to recommend and lock appropriate tools and frameworks, balancing speed, reliability, future integrations and long-term scalability.




The Role

You will act as the technical lead for the software system, working closely with:

• the Founder (product, UX/UI and interaction design)
• a hardware engineer responsible for device electronics and firmware

This senior role requires a minimum of 5-7 years of experience.

The role focuses on refining the MVP architecture, improving reliability, and preparing the software for manufacturing and real-world deployment.

This position suits an engineer comfortable turning prototypes into reliable products and building systems designed to evolve over time.




Responsibilities
System Architecture

Refine and stabilise the end-to-end system architecture, including:

• application layer
• device communication layer
• supporting backend services where required

Define clearly what logic runs:

• on the device firmware
• in the application layer
• in backend services

Ensure the architecture allows for future integrations with additional technologies, devices and services.

Document architecture decisions and technical trade-offs.


Application Development

Improve and stabilise the existing MVP application.

Responsibilities include:

• implementing core interaction logic
• improving system reliability
• supporting tactile, non-visual and voice-assisted interaction patterns
• collaborating closely with product design

The focus is functionality and stability rather than consumer polish.




Hardware ↔ Software Integration

Work with the hardware engineer to define and implement the communication layer between the device and the application, including:

• BLE or similar communication protocols
• command structures
• state handling and synchronisation
• debugging and reliability tools




AI & Voice Integration

Support integration of voice-assisted AI features that allow users to interact with the system through spoken input and audio feedback.

Ensure the architecture can support future AI capabilities and integrations.




Must Have Experience

• Experience working with hardware-connected products
• Experience integrating software with physical devices (BLE, firmware interfaces or similar)
• Experience building companion applications, including:
– native mobile applications
– cross-platform mobile apps
– web applications wrapped as mobile apps

• Strong system architecture and integration skills
• Experience taking prototypes toward production-ready systems




Contractor Requirements

• Must operate as an independent contractor with a valid ABN
• Must be able to invoice for contract work
• Must hold Professional Indemnity Insurance
• Must hold Public Liability Insurance
• Sydney-based preferred for limited in-person collaboration


Deliverables

By the end of the engagement we expect:

• a stable integrated software system connected to the device
• reliable hardware–software communication
• improved stability and usability of the MVP application
• software suitable for manufacturing preparation and pilot testing
• architecture documented to support future integrations and scaling




Hiring Process

Screening call

Interview

Technical interview + paid task (2-3hours)

Start engagement

We are looking for someone available to start immediately.




How to Apply

Please send:

• CV / Resume
• Portfolio or relevant project examples - ideally hardware & software
• GitHub


IP & Legal

All intellectual property created during the engagement remains the property of the company.

A standard NDA and contractor agreement will be provided prior to commencement.
